Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC154 details decomposing a requirement and post-conditions about temporary links and event messages, but contains no mention of storage or management in a 3rd-party RM tool (e.g., DOORS), no import/export/sync operations, nor assertions that requirements are persisted or managed in such a tool.

Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC155 describes refining a requirement and establishing temporary links; there is no evidence of interaction with or management in a 3rd-party requirements management tool like DOORS.

Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC156 covers modifying a requirement and changing link status, but does not reference or verify requirements being managed in a 3rd-party RM tool (DOORS) or related integration behaviors.

Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC157 defines deletion behavior (setting obsolete status and tentative link status) but contains no assertions about management or storage of requirements in an external 3rd-party RM tool such as DOORS.

Description: RQ100 Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reason: TC158 specifies merging requirements and link reassignment, but does not mention or assert that requirements are managed by a 3rd-party RM tool (DOORS) or that merge operations are coordinated with such a tool.

Reason: TC142 describes that a non-registered subscriber attempts to establish a trace between a UML artifact and a requirement and that no trace is established. While it references a UML artifact (which is a non-requirement artifact type), it contains no assertion about management of non-requirement artifacts in 3rd-party tools (Rational Rose/Clear case) or integration with such tools.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC154 covers decomposing a requirement into sub-requirements and resulting temporary links and events. It is focused on requirement artifacts and does not address management of non-requirement artifacts in 3rd-party tools.

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC155 addresses refining a requirement and establishing temporary links. It concerns requirement artifacts and events; it does not assert any behavior related to management of non-requirement artifacts in 3rd-party tools (e.g., Rational Rose, Clear case).

Description: RQ101 Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reason: TC157 covers deletion of a requirement and related link state changes. It addresses requirement lifecycle and link status but provides no evidence of managing non-requirement artifacts in external tools or interacting with Rational Rose/Clear case.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test exercises the positive case: a registered subscriber successfully establishes a trace, directly supporting the requirement's allow clause. Missing: does not assert enforcement against unregistered actors or boundary/error behaviours, so confidence is partial.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test explicitly exercises an unregistered subscriber attempting to create a trace and asserts that no trace is established, directly supporting the requirement's prohibition clause. It demonstrates the negative behaviour (unregistered prevented). Missing: does not show handling of edge cases (e.g., revoked registrations or manager scope), so confidence is partial.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test focuses on successful subscriber registration as a postcondition, but does not exercise or assert trace establishment or the restriction that only registered subscribers may establish traces. It is supportive but does not directly verify the trace access control.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test concerns subscriber manager registration with an event server; it does not exercise subscriber trace establishment or the permission restriction in the requirement.

Description: RQ102 Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reason: This test deals with event notification storage for registered subscribers; it does not assert or verify any behaviour about trace establishment or access control for establishing traces.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.

It verifies behavior when registration is not completed, but it does not assert success of registration (the positive requirement). It therefore provides partial coverage of the registration-related behaviors (error handling) but not full positive verification.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: TC147 is about event publication and subscriber manager subscriptions; it does not contain assertions that a user registers as a subscriber.

Description: RQ103 A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reason: TC149 deals with event notifications to a subscriber manager and storage in event log; it does not assert user registration behavior.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: TC144 describes a negative scenario where the subscriber fails to register and is removed. It does not assert or verify that on registration the subscriber is registered under the control of a subscriber manager. Therefore it does not provide positive evidence for RQ104.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: TC146 concerns the subscriber manager registering with an event server. It does not exercise or assert subscriber registration under a subscriber manager, so it does not verify RQ104.

Description: RQ104 On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: TC147 is about real-time event publication and the subscriber manager's subscription; it does not assert that a subscriber, upon registration, is registered under the control of a subscriber manager, so it does not verify RQ104.

Reason: TC149 addresses receipt and storage of non-speculative event notifications and does not exercise or assert subscriber registration under a subscriber manager; it therefore does not verify RQ104.

Reason: TC150 assumes as a precondition that the subscriber manager has registered subscribers but focuses on event notification processing. It does not assert the act of registration itself or verify that registration results in control by the subscriber manager, so it does not satisfy RQ104.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: TC141 explicitly describes the user/subscriber action of establishing a trace between a UML artifact and a requirement and asserts the postcondition that a trace is established. This directly aligns semantically with the requirement's core behavior (establishing traces via subscription).

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: TC142 provides a negative/error scenario showing that an unregistered subscriber cannot establish a trace. Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement

This demonstrates behavior resulting from an existing subscription (notification), supporting the subscription concept, but it does not assert the act of establishing the subscription/trace itself. Therefore it is only partially relevant with weak confidence.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: TC154 focuses on decomposition of requirements and temporary requirement-to-requirement links. It does not address artifact subscriptions or artifact-to-requirement trace establishment.

Description: RQ105 The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reason: TC155 concerns requirement refinement and temporary links between requirements (requirement-to-requirement links). It does not assert establishing a subscription from an artifact to a requirement or artifact-to-requirement traces, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: TC147 describes an event publication and assumes a subscriber manager is subscribed; it does not assert placement of arbitrary artifacts under subscriber manager control, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager

Description: RQ106 Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reason: TC164 concerns event processing order and prohibition of out-of-order processing. It does not assert that artifacts are placed under the control of a subscriber manager, so it does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reason: TC143 verifies subscriber registration with a subscriber manager; it does not exercise or assert subscription deletion.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.

Description: RQ107 The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: This test concerns a subscriber registering with a subscriber manager, not the subscriber manager registering with the event server. It does not verify the requirement.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.

It does not itself assert that registration enables receiving messages, so confidence is partial (registration verified; linkage to receiving is covered by other tests).

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reason: The test verifies that, upon coming online, a subscriber manager downloads missed notifications from the event server (covers receipt/recovery). It does not explicitly test the act of registering itself with the eventserver, so it is a partial match.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.

Description: RQ108 A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.

Description: RQ109 If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.

Description: RQ110 Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.

Description: RQ111 A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.

Description: RQ112 A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.

However, it does not explicitly state that the subscriber manager parsed the message to determine its type or that it differentiates between types at receipt time vs relying on upstream classification; therefore coverage is partial.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.

Description: RQ113 On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.

Reason: The test lists the running trigger driver as a precondition and expects an event message to be sent when adding a requirement, directly supporting the requirement's intent. As a descriptive test case it covers the happy path but not boundary/error behaviors of the driver.

However, it is a high-level test description (precondition/postcondition) and does not exercise driver initialization, failure modes, or non-happy paths, so coverage of the full requirement behavior is partial.

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.

Description: RQ120 The prototype system shall utilize a driver to trigger events.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: This test asserts that adding a new requirement results in a new event message being sent. It demonstrates that requirement changes generate event messages but does not assert that the eventserver receives them, so it is partial evidence toward the requirement.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: This test explicitly asserts that when a requirement is modified, a modify event message is sent. That demonstrates the 'when a requirement changes -> event message is produced' side, but it does not assert that the eventserver receives that message, so it only partially addresses the requirement.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.
Reason: This test asserts that deleting a requirement results in a delete event message being sent. It covers the 'requirement changes -> event message sent' aspect but does not demonstrate that the eventserver receives the message, so it is partial evidence.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.

Description: RQ127 The eventserver shall receive event messages when a requirement changes.

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.

Description: RQ128 On receipt of an event message the eventserver shall record the event in an eventlog.

Description: RQ129 On receipt of an event; the eventserver shall identify all relevant subscribers for the event.

Description: RQ129 On receipt of an event; the eventserver shall identify all relevant subscribers for the event.

Description: RQ131 When an evolutionary event occurs; the eventserver shall notify subscribers of the event.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.

Description: RQ138 The user shall update related artifacts according to the information contained in the event messages.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
Reason: This test verifies identification of impacted requirements and subscribers. It does not assert elimination or cleanup of temporary traces or tentative links.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
Reason: This test concerns processing order of event messages and prohibits out-of-order processing; it does not assert any elimination or cleanup of temporary traces or tentative links.

Description: RQ139 The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
Reason: This test explicitly exercises user-driven elimination of tentative links and asserts that links are changed per user direction and that event messages generated from now-eliminated tentative links are removed from the event processing list and display. Those assertions directly implement the requirement's core behavior (eliminate unnecessary temporary traces and clean up related event messages).

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC155 describes creation of temporary links when refining a requirement. It documents establishment of temporary links but contains no assertion that events become automatically eliminated when those temporary links/traces are removed.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC157 describes deletion of a requirement leading to existing links becoming tentative. It does not assert that events are eliminated when temporary traces are later eliminated.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC164 addresses processing order prohibitions and that no events are processed when attempted out of order. It does not assert removal of events when temporary traces are eliminated or any automatic elimination behavior.

Description: RQ140 The notification processing system shall automatically eliminate any events that are not longer valid as a result of the user eliminating temporary traces.
Reason: TC165 explicitly states that when the user discards tentative links, additional event messages previously sent on the basis of those tentative links are removed from the event processing list and display. This matches the core behavior (elimination of invalid events resulting from the elimination of temporary traces). However, the test does not exercise or assert all aspects: 'automatically' timing/trigger semantics, coverage of 'any events' (all event types), concurrency/error handling, and boundary conditions are not shown.
